questions.question: In anaerobic respiration, glucose is broken down into what?
questions.explanation: Glucose breaks down into ethyl alcohol, carbon dioxide, and energy. Glucose → Alcohol + Carbon dioxide + Energy

questions.question: Sea urchins are an example of __.
questions.explanation: Sea urchins are an example of Echinodermata. Echinoderms are spiny-skinned animals. They have Radial symmetry and triploblastic.

questions.question: Which of the following are included in Kingdom Animals?
questions.explanation: Kingdom Animalia includes all multicellular, eukaryotic, heterotrophic organisms. They reproduce sexually and asexually.

questions.question: What is the appearance of the most advanced character in a crocodile?
questions.explanation: The four-chambered heart is the most advanced character in a crocodile which makes them different from other reptiles. The four-chambered heart is characteristic of the mammalian class. The presence of this type of heart makes crocodiles similar to mammals.

questions.question: Which of the following group of mammals, the placenta is absent?
questions.explanation: Prototheria group of mammals in which the placenta is absent. Prototheria is a subclass to which the orders Monotremata.

questions.question: Which of the following mammal lays eggs?
questions.explanation: There are only two types of egg-laying mammals left on the planet today, the duck-billed platypus and the echidna.

questions.question: Glycerol is a byproduct of animal fat used in the production of?
questions.explanation: Glycerol is a byproduct of animal fat used in the production of toothpaste, antifreeze, plastics, and paints.

questions.question: Nissl granules present in neurons are made up of?
questions.explanation: Nissl granules consist of rough endoplasmic reticulum, a collection of parallel, membrane-bound cisternae studded with ribosomes on the cytosolic surface of the membranes.

questions.question: What is the function of the optic nerve?

options.label: A
options.text: relays signals between the brain and the eyes

options.label: B
options.text: relays signals between the brain and the ears

options.label: C
options.text: relays signals between the brain and the skin

options.label: D
options.text: relays signals between the brain and the tongue

answer.label: A
answer.text: relays signals between the brain and the eyes

questions.explanation: The optic nerve relays signals between the brain and the eyes. It acts as the agent of vision.

questions.question: The branch of biology that studies the immune system in all organisms.
questions.explanation: Immunology is a branch of biology that studies the immune system in all organisms.
